data.description: Iraqi Oil Minister tells Kurdistan's Rudaw: Oil exports via Turkey Ceyhan pipeline to resume at approximately 180,000-190,000 bpd.

data.title: Bitcoin lags in DeFi market at $7.9bn while DeFi market hits $148bn, with Ethereum leading with $90bn in assets.
data.description: Bitcoin, the largest and original cryptocurrency, is surprisingly absent in the decentralized finance (DeFi) space. DeFi has become a $148 billion market, with $50 billion in blockchain lending alone. Ethereum dominates with over $90 billion locked in smart contracts. In contrast, Bitcoin only holds $7.9 billion, trailing behind Solana and Binance's BNB chain. Despite its high liquidity, Bitcoin remains largely idle in DeFi projects, leading analysts and institutions to question its potential beyond cold storage. The issue lies in Bitcoin's intentionally limited scripting language, which hinders its ability to support complex self-executing contracts. This highlights a missed opportunity for both DeFi projects and Bitcoin holders in terms of yield generation. Institutions are now exploring ways to address this inefficiency in Bitcoin's design.

data.title: Nidec Corp announces key disclosures on fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, including financial statements correction.
data.description: Nidec Corporation, based in Kyoto, Japan, has released important updates today related to its fiscal year ending on March 31, 2025. The company disclosed the submission of the securities report and internal control report, along with a partial correction of the financial statements summary. These disclosures are crucial for shareholders and investors. Nidec Corporation aims to keep all stakeholders informed about its operations and financial status. This communication is part of the company's commitment to transparency and accountability.

data.title: Kuaishou Technology launches Kling AI 2.5 Turbo Video Model with text and image capabilities, boasting industry-leading performance and reduced utilization costs.
data.description: In Hong Kong on September 26, 2025, Kuaishou Technology revealed the rollout of its Kling AI 2.5 Turbo Video Model on September 23. This model, marked by major enhancements to its text-to-video and image-to-video functions, boasts industry-leading performance and heightened generation quality. Notably, this updated version also focuses on cost reductions, making it more affordable for users. Kuaishou, known for being a prominent content community and social platform, operates under the HKD Counter Stock Code: 01024 and RMB Counter Stock Code: 81024.

data.title: Deep EigenMatics in Texas gets US Patent for AI method in drug discovery and protein design.
data.description: In Sugar Land, Texas on September 26, 2025, Deep EigenMatics, a startup specializing in pioneering Artificial Intelligence techniques for drug discovery and development, has secured a United States Patent for a groundbreaking innovation in AI-driven drug discovery and protein design.

data.title: StatLab launches Diapath equipment in the US, fulfilling 2024 acquisition plan to provide innovative histology instruments to labs.
data.description: In McKinney, Texas, StatLab Medical Products, a prominent provider of pathology supplies, has revealed the official launch of Diapath signature Pathology instruments in the U.S. This launch comes after StatLab's acquisition of Diapath in 2024, as part of their strategic plan to introduce unique histology instrumentation to American laboratories and provide a complete pathology workflow solution. The innovative instruments are now available for commercial use, marking a significant development in the field.

data.title: Tokenized sterling deposits pilot starts with 6 major UK banks - Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds Banking Group, in collaboration with UK Finance.
data.description: UK Finance has initiated a pilot program for tokenized deposits in collaboration with six leading banks, including Barclays, HSBC, and Lloyds Banking Group.

data.title: LDC's 2025 report reveals ongoing underrepresentation of U.S. Latinos in media streaming, broadcast, and cable platforms.
data.description: In Los Angeles, LDC has published the 2025 U.S. Latinos in Media Report™, highlighting ongoing underrepresentation of Latinos across streaming, broadcast, and cable platforms. The report sheds light on the disparity in media representation, emphasizing the need for more inclusive portrayals. Key findings include significant underrepresentation of Latinos in both on-screen and off-screen roles. This report brings attention to the importance of diversity in media and calls for increased efforts to address the lack of representation.
